summary: A one-file stage decomp matching PR for `src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c` changed `grInishie1_801FBC4C` by introducing a local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` before calling `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP`. The PR body says this fixed register allocation and matched the function to 100%; the bot report confirms one new match for `grInishie1_801FBC4C`, moving that item from 93.85% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains `grInishie1_801FBC4C` and the local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` extraction before `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP`.

what_changed.text: Replaced direct macro use `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2)` with a local `HSD_JObj* jobj2 = gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2;` followed by `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(jobj2);`.
No other source file changes are present in the slice.
The PR body explicitly attributes the match to introducing local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` to fix register allocation.
The bot report lists one new match: `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` item `grInishie1_801FBC4C`, +10 bytes, 93.85% before and 100.00% after.

smart_moves.text: Kept the edit narrowly scoped to one temporary variable in the target function rather than changing surrounding control flow, data, headers, or macros.
Used a typed local `HSD_JObj*` instead of raw pointer arithmetic or an untyped temporary.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a macro call that takes a nested typed field expression, extracting the field into a typed local can affect MWCC register allocation enough to close a match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: local `HSD_JObj* jobj2` fixed register allocation
raw_diff: direct field expression changed to local before macro call

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small local-lifetime changes can be valid matching tactics when the PR records the intended codegen effect and a narrow report confirms the target symbol matched.

naming_conventions.name: jobj2
naming_conventions.notes: The name mirrors the existing field name `jobj2`; no broader naming rule is proven by this PR alone.

naming_conventions.name: gp
naming_conventions.meaning: Ground pointer returned by `GET_GROUND(gobj)`; pre-existing in the function context shown by the diff.
naming_conventions.notes: Consistent with loaded conservative-naming guidance for short role names, but this PR did not introduce `gp`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a typed local for a nested struct field before passing it to a macro to influence register allocation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: Bot report says `grInishie1_801FBC4C` reached 100.00% and was one new match.
cautions: This is a narrow register-allocation tactic, not a general rule to add locals everywhere.
The slice does not include assembly or objdiff output beyond the bot report.

review_feedback.text: Author said they picked something small to test the waters and asked to be told if anything was missed.
decomp-dev bot reported one new match for GALE01: `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` / `grInishie1_801FBC4C`, +10 bytes, 93.85% before, 100.00% after.
No review comments or human review records are present in the loaded slice.

follow_up_queries: DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP nested field local variable match

notes: Evidence is strong for the exact changed file, diff, stated tactic, and reported match result.
The loaded slice has no review comments, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
The PR body claims a 100% match and the bot report corroborates the target item reaching 100.00%, but the slice does not include raw objdiff or assembly.
Broader guidance about local temporaries and register allocation should remain scoped because this PR contains only one small example.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed local before macro call can fix register allocation
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2556, `grInishie1_801FBC4C` matched after replacing `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2)` with a typed local `HSD_JObj* jobj2 = gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2;` and then `DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP(jobj2);`. The author stated this fixed register allocation, and the bot report listed the function as one new 100.00% match.

source_update_candidates.title: Mention narrow typed local extraction as a register-allocation tactic when verified
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a small note under matching tactics: when a macro receives a nested typed field expression, extracting that expression into a correctly typed local can be a legitimate register-allocation tactic if narrow objdiff/checkdiff or bot evidence shows it closes the target match.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards cover typed access and evidence-backed tactics, but this PR provides a concrete example of a typed local introduced specifically for register allocation before a macro call.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that every nested macro argument should be extracted into a local.
Do not infer any reviewer preference because the loaded slice contains no human review comments.
Do not claim adjacent-function regression checks or raw objdiff details beyond the bot report; they are not included in the evidence.
